Online Quotes Available: You can easily get estimates for your premiums using HDFC’s online plan calculators, without needing an agent.
Wide Range of Plan Options: HDFC offers many different individual life insurance policies, including plans tailored for specific audiences (such as women or rural residents).
Credibility and Claim Settlement Ratio: HDFC has an established track record and a high claim settlement ratio, which indicates efficient claim processing.
review-details_isCollapsed__Bsg_9 2:
Limited Options for Non-Residents: HDFC primarily caters to Indian residents, and while they do offer some policies for non-resident Indians (NRIs), the options are limited.
Different Policy Structures: HDFC’s policies may differ significantly from U.S.-based life insurance plans. For instance, some child plans provide money for specific milestones like education or starting a new job.
Complex Information: With numerous life insurance options, HDFC’s website can be overwhelming, and finding specific information may require contacting customer service or an insurance agent.

I worked here for almost 10 months and quit recently. I learned so much about life and career while working here. My experience here was a lot different than all my previous work experiences. This is a fantastic place to work. Work in this place if and only if:
1. You can't speak English and don't have the smartness to learn it as well.
2. You have absolutely no understanding of finance and investment.
3. If you are a slave of money, so much that you are ready to cheat customers and steal their hard earned money to earn incentives.
4. Work here if you will never buy their investment products yourself but would love to recommend it to others.
5. Work here if you feel lying to people who trust you and actually seek your help about banking and investment is your birth right.
6. Work here if you are dumb enough to not realize that there are a lot of great things to do in your career and life than selling ULIPs.
7.Work here if you know deep down inside you that you cannot do anything else.
8. Last but not the least, work here if you truly believe that you are a loser.

If you don't have the above qualities, then unfortunately this isn't the right place for you.

review-details_isCollapsed__Bsg_9 2:
1. For sales specially in Bancassurance ( largest business generating channel of HDFC Life) you've to deal with HDFC bank's employees. Being a third party provider it's very difficult for you to keep your self respect. You have to deal with humiliation everyday as unlike other Third Party Provider( like Demat, Credit cards etc.) you've to be in the branch for the whole day.
2. The Support system is very poor. In fact the resource management is pathetic. A fresher who's not well versed with system and intricate documentation is given responsibility of a big branch or many a times he has to handle two branches simultaneously. 
3. Your personal development is tends to nil. No learning and development. It's a pure sales role and that too in retail. 
4. Quality of people with whom you're dealing is very very poor. After MBA from reputed college working as a Management Trainee in sales would make you cry for sure. More than 90% Management Trainees have quit in the span of 4 years.

review-details_isCollapsed__Bsg_9 2:
Priorities are more towards pleasing management rather than doing your job and that is slowly communicated to you over time. Because of this, a culture exists, where everyone wastes time trying to pin the blame on someone else so that they do not get into trouble with their manager. As a result, there is less work done and more pointing fingers. 

If you want to go anywhere in this organisation, learn powerpoint and excel. The company thrives on ppts and excel sheets. Managers will applaud you for your slick ppt/reporting skills. Expect no such praise for your actual job.

Part of the HDFC Life culture also includes not doing your job unless asked over and over again. You will wonder why people don’t do their jobs but this is what everyone in the company is taught - Focus on making ppts as opposed to doing your actual job. Truth is... Nobody gets credit for completing their work on time so why work?

There is an unusual amount of manual work. This is again not a value-add activity.
